items.title: Driver 'didn't have time to brake' before crash that killed boy on scooter

children.content: An 11-year-old boy dies after his scooter collides with a car in Mandurah, with a witness saying they saw the boy riding down a small hill onto the road before the crash — WA's fifth road fatality over the Easter period.

gallery.altText: Flowers and a teddy bear sit on a limestone wall.
A group of holiday villas with a short drive way leading up to them.
A close-up shot of a short drive way sloping up to enter a car park from a residential street, with a 'private property' sign.

thumbnail.altText: Flowers and a teddy bear sit on a limestone wall.
A group of holiday villas with a short drive way leading up to them.
A close-up shot of a short drive way sloping up to enter a car park from a residential street, with a 'private property' sign.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.

items.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death

children.content: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

image.altText: Rohan Dennis wearing formal attire walks along a sandstone wall, with a broadcast camera partially visible.

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears Cyclone Narelle might be a climate disaster that results in a "double whammy" for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

gallery.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.
A dead turtle hatchling is held aloft, still in its broken shell.
An aerial image of a medium-sized boat floating on a blue-green reef with snorkelers in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.
A dead turtle hatchling is held aloft, still in its broken shell.
An aerial image of a medium-sized boat floating on a blue-green reef with snorkelers in the foreground.

items.title: Owner of dog that mauled young girl charged over attack

children.content: The owner of a dog that savaged a girl in a Perth suburb, leaving her with serious facial injuries, is charged under Western Australia's Dog Act.

image.altText: A residential street in the Perth suburb of Hillarys on a sunny day, with houses on both sides of the road.

items.title: 'No one else should go through this': Young job hunters a target in scam surge

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

image.altText: A man is scrolling on his phone.

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers in Perth reporting a surge in sales.

articles.title: All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands

children.content: The FuelWatch reporting scheme is being expanded to all regional fuel stations in Western Australia, with about 200 additional retailers required to report their fuel prices.

image.altText: Fuel pumps and screens depicting prices for petrol and diesal at a service station.

gallery.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.
A row of e-bikes outside a cycle shop in Perth, with a fuel price board at a petrol station in the background.
A sign on the ground outside a bike shop reading "beat the fuel prices, e-bike sale now on".

thumbnail.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.
A row of e-bikes outside a cycle shop in Perth, with a fuel price board at a petrol station in the background.
A sign on the ground outside a bike shop reading "beat the fuel prices, e-bike sale now on".

items.title: ASX up 1.3pc after modest gains on Wall St as Iran deadline looms

children.content: The Australian share market is up 1.35pc after a four-day long weekend, as traders appear to look through the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

items.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

image.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

items.title: Artemis II moon flyby breaks Apollo 13’s distance record

children.content: The astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, capturing never-before-seen views of the far side of the Moon.

gallery.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.

thumbnail.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.
AN image of the Moon, as seen from space.
Earth's Moon, as seen from a spacecraft.

items.title: Pheobe Bishop murder-accused to face court on anniversary of 17yo's disappearance

children.content: The case of a woman accused of murdering Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop has been adjourned to May 15 — the first anniversary of Pheobe's disappearance.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman with red hair

items.title: 'Grow up': Meta censoring warnings about illicit drugs, health experts say

children.content: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

image.altText: a small orange and grey pill next to a ruler

items.title: I am tired of constantly defending being single and child-free

children.content: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

image.altText: Julie stands in a green dress, smiling.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: A toy rabbit sits in a field of tulips

items.title: Angry WA customers among those who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse want ASIC probe

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers join a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

items.title: Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners

children.content: The Northern Territory government has introduced legislation intended to reform Australia's de facto online gambling regulator, but it's been criticised as a "damage-control exercise".

image.altText: A person holding a mobile phone in their hand, with a sports betting app open on the phone.

items.title: Voss says struggling Carlton focused to 'get on with the task'

children.content: Carlton coach Michael Voss insists he is ignoring most of the intense scrutiny on his embattled AFL club, adamant key stakeholders remain on the same page.

image.altText: Carlton Blues head coach Michael Voss leads the team off the field

items.title: Fuel thieves targeting NSW truckers as they sleep

children.content: The owner of a trucking company in Western Sydney says most cases of fuel theft have occurred when his drivers have parked on the side of the road for the night.

image.altText: Hand holding petrol cap with cap inside tank

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

image.altText: A satellite image of a large amount of cloud cover in a circle on a map

items.title: WA's unusual 'Easter eggs' captured in aerial images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

gallery.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

thumbnail.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.
